title: Hadoop Cheat Sheet
description: Essential Hadoop CLI commands and configurations for engineers.

commands.description: List files and directories in HDFS root directory
commands.scenario: Use when you need to see the contents of the HDFS root directory.

commands.description: Upload a file from local filesystem to HDFS
commands.scenario: Use when you need to upload data to HDFS for processing.

commands.description: Download a file from HDFS to local filesystem
commands.scenario: Use when you need to retrieve processed data from HDFS.
Use when you need to delete unnecessary files from HDFS to free up space.

commands.description: Create a new directory in HDFS
commands.scenario: Use when you need to organize files in HDFS by creating directories.

commands.description: Run a Hadoop job using a JAR file
commands.scenario: Use when you need to execute a MapReduce job on your data.

commands.description: Check the health of the HDFS
commands.scenario: Use when you need to diagnose issues with HDFS.

commands.description: Get a report on HDFS status
commands.scenario: Use when you need a detailed status report of your HDFS cluster.

commands.description: Change permissions of a file or directory in HDFS
commands.scenario: Use when you need to modify access permissions for HDFS files or directories.

commands.description: Copy a file from local filesystem to HDFS
commands.scenario: Use when you need to copy files from local to HDFS for processing.

commands.description: Copy a file from HDFS to local filesystem
commands.scenario: Use when you need to copy files from HDFS to local for analysis.

commands.description: Display disk usage of HDFS directory
commands.scenario: Use when you need to check how much space a directory is using in HDFS.

commands.description: Display contents of a file in HDFS
commands.scenario: Use when you need to quickly view the contents of a file stored in HDFS.
